title: The History of Photography
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The (0) invention of photography in the 19th century changed how people recorded the world around them. Early cameras were (17) expensive and required long exposure times, but they produced images of (18) extraordinary detail. Over time, technological (19) improvements made photography more (20) accessible to ordinary people.||The (21) introduction of digital cameras in the late twentieth century transformed the industry once again. Today, millions of (22) photographs are taken every day, often using smartphones. This has created an (23) unprecedented visual record of human experience and sparked new debates about (24) privacy and the ethics of image sharing.

example.text: The (0) INVENTION of photography in the 19th century changed how people recorded the world around them. (INVENT)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'expensive' is formed from 'expense' by replacing '-e' with '-ive'.
The adjective 'extraordinary' combines the prefix 'extra-' with 'ordinary', meaning beyond the usual.
The plural noun 'improvements' is formed from 'improve' by adding '-ment' and '-s'.
The adjective 'accessible' is formed from 'access' by adding the suffix '-ible'.
The noun 'introduction' is formed from 'introduce' by replacing '-ce' with '-ction'.
The plural noun 'photographs' is formed from 'photograph' by adding '-s'.
The adjective 'unprecedented' uses the prefix 'un-' combined with 'precedent' + '-ed'.
The noun 'privacy' is formed from 'private' by replacing '-ate' with '-acy'.
